description.p: Sparkamp is an open-source, keyboard-centric audio player for Linux and GNOME inspired by the classic Winamp interface. It supports MP3, OGG, FLAC and other common audio formats, and includes a full-featured 10-band equalizer, a media library, visualizer plugins, and custom CSS skinning.
Launch with sparkamp for the GTK4 graphical interface or sparkamp --tui for the terminal (TUI) mode.

Granite becomes a built-in visualizer. The Geiss-inspired plasma now lives in the core (no separate plugin) with a warp-map feedback engine: 8 palettes, 12 warp effects, beat-reactive palette and effect rolling, and a flux beat detector that surfaces BPM and time signature. Press 'a' to cycle to Granite, 'e' for a random effect, 'f' or double-click for fullscreen; Settings exposes speed, palette, feedback, effect, and auto-switch, and credits the original by Ryan Geiss. The same renderer drives the GTK and macOS frontends under a single-driver rule so the mini and fullscreen views stay in sync, and the fullscreen 'g' overlay shows FPS, BPM, and meter.

Large internal refactor: the monolithic core and frontend files were split into focused modules (FFI, media library, shared tag/text/time helpers, and the TUI and macOS sources by domain) with no behavior change. Playlist handling now preserves a track's real, accessible path instead of substituting a stale catalogue row, so saved tracks no longer appear missing and unavailable files show the correct status; the ID3 editor gains an explicit "(undefined)" genre option and a selectable filename header.

Playlist editor overhaul. The specific-playlist view now uses a ColumnView mirroring the files-view columns (visibility, widths, order share the same config and refresh on every playlist load), gains a position column with canonical play-order tracking that survives duplicate entries, and an artwork View button to match the files view. Right-click menus across the active playlist, files view, and editor gain a unified Add to Playlist submenu with New Playlist… plus every saved playlist; Edit ID3 hides on multi-select; the editor's Remove now removes from the playlist (rewriting the M3U8) rather than the library DB. Multi-select drag-and-drop in the active playlist preserves the selection across GTK's transient collapse and reorders all dragged rows, with drop-position honoring before/after halves. Drag-reorder in the editor is gated to position-ASC sort. Several smaller fixes: BOM-prefixed M3U8 files parse correctly, default save directory falls back through first ML folder / ~/Music / home, save errors surface in a modal dialog, the artwork viewer reports decode failures inline, Delete key removes from the editor, the info-button glyph matches the surrounding mode-buttons, and a new crash log at ~/.config/sparkamp/crash.log captures panics + GLib criticals on Linux so silent crashes leave a trace.

First stable release. Adds macOS DMG build, GTK skin system with 14-variable CSS theming, media library polish (mode-button toggle states, locked column widths, repeat/shuffle text labels), visualizer width stability across player states, and CI pipelines that produce both a Linux Flatpak and a macOS DMG from every commit to main.

GTK4 is now the default interface (launch with sparkamp); TUI is available via sparkamp --tui. Adds media library with background scanning, spectrum visualizer with configurable color zones and mirror mode, bar visualizer improvements, shuffle persistence, and numerous GTK fixes including jump-window search, scroll-to-row, and replace-mode autoplay.
Initial public release featuring GStreamer audio backend, GTK4 GUI, TUI mode, 10-band EQ with pre-amp, media library, visualizer plugins, and CSS skinning.
